name: Solo restaker (severe)
recommendations: SEVERE slashing exposure: expected annual loss ~4.86% with a worst-case haircut of ~100%. Reduce or diversify this position.
High operator concentration: a single operator controls a large share of the stake, so a correlated fault could slash most of the position at once.
Single validator: the entire stake sits behind one validator with no diversification against a slashing event.
Elevated correlated-fault probability: the tail dominates (~99% of expected loss). Double-sign / correlated penalties are large and hard to recover.
Restaking amplifies the slashing surface (x2.40): the same stake is exposed to multiple AVS slashing conditions simultaneously.
Effectively uninsured: ~4.86% expected loss is borne directly with no coverage offset.
Large worst-case haircut (~100%): a single correlated event could remove a substantial fraction of the position.

name: Diversified LST (low)
recommendations: Low slashing exposure: expected annual loss ~0.03%. Risk is contained but not zero.
Well diversified across validators and operators, which limits the correlated-slashing haircut to a small slice of the position.
